title: Storyboard · "Knock" — Radio→Animation production script · S14
body:
**Scene 14: 17:30–18:30 · The Photograph**

HERO FRAME: Close on a framed black-and-white photograph of an elderly woman on a wooden dresser · 1920s clothing, gentle smile · the silver frame catching warm lamplight · a small vase of dried wildflowers next to it · Walter's hand (only hand) on the frame

MOTION: kling — Walter's hand lifts the photograph slightly, turns it, sets it back down. The lamplight glints on the silver frame as it moves. Dust motes catch the light.

MOOD: Held love, the comfort of the small remaining proof.

AUDIO: Walter: *"I have a photograph of my mother. She died before all this began. I took her photograph from her apartment and put it on my dresser. I look at it every day."*

source.label: Dimension X episode #05 (NBC Radio, May 6, 1950) adapting Fredric Brown's 1948 short-short story "Knock" (originally in *Thrilling Wonder Stories*, December 1948)

author: matt

witnesses.label: Rufinus of Aquileia, Commentary on the Apostles' Creed (c. AD 404)
Pirminius, De singulis libris canonicis scarapsus (c. AD 750) — earliest received form
Cited by every major catechism since 8th century
